# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Kubernetes Deployment that pulls our application image from Docker Hub. Use the latest tag so we always get the newest version automatically. This is for our development environment where we want continuous updates without manual intervention.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: app-deployment
  namespace: development
  labels:
    app: my-application
    environment: development
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-application
      environment: development
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-application
        environment: development
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app-container
          image: myorganization/my-application:latest
          # imagePullPolicy is set to Always so Kubernetes pulls the newest
          # image on every pod restart even though the tag hasn't changed.
          # This is critical when using :latest to ensure updates are picked up.
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8080
              protocol: TCP
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 128Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 512Mi
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 5
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /healthz
              port: 8080
            initialDelaySeconds: 15
            periodSeconds: 10
      # If pulling from a private Docker Hub repository, uncomment below
      # imagePullSecrets:
      #   - name: dockerhub-credentials
      restartPolicy: Always
